name: Litanies Trail
name_original: Sendero de las Letanias por la Ermita Talako Ama — circular desde Ea
description: Jog this moderate 3.3-mile circular route from Ea, gaining 588 feet of elevation with coastal views and the Ermita Talako Ama.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ea offer a unique blend of coastal beauty and natural park landscapes within the Basque Country. The region is characterized by its picturesque location within a creek, featuring a serene, lagoon-like beach and proximity to the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. Runners can explore diverse terrain including marshes, winding rivers, dense oak and beech forests, and dramatic rugged coastlines. Best jogging routes around Ea The most popular jogging route is Litanies Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes about 42 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Laga Beach – Laga Beach loop from Ibarrangelu, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This route provides refreshing sea air and scenic views along the coast. Local runners also love the Talaia loop from Elantxobe, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through green mountains and coastal vistas, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging around Ea is defined by coastal paths, dense forests, and green mountains, offering a picturesque backdrop for any run. The network of routes provides options for various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging climbs. The routes in Ea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,800 runners have used komoot to explore Ea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Ea?
A: There are over 120 dedicated running routes around Ea, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences. These include 7 easy routes, 61 moderate routes, and 57 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Ea?
A: Jogging routes in Ea offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find picturesque coastal paths with refreshing sea air, trails winding through dense oak and beech forests, and paths leading up green mountains. The region is part of the &lt;strong&gt;Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;, so expect varied landscapes including marshes and river valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Ea offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otoio loop from Ispaster-Elexalde&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.6 km (11.6 miles) route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia (276 m) – Elantxobe Cemetery loop from Ibarrangelu&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.7 km (6 miles) with challenging ascents.

Q: What are some scenic points of interest I can see while running near Ea?
A: Many running routes in Ea pass by beautiful natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artzubi Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;. The unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6628930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oma Forest&lt;/a&gt;, with its painted trees, is also accessible via walking paths in the region.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Ea area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Ea are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31894464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Laga Beach – Laga Beach loop from Ibarrangelu&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.3 km (8.3 miles) coastal path. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otoio loop from Ispaster-Elexalde&lt;/a&gt; is another circular option for those seeking a more difficult run.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Ea?
A: The jogging routes in Ea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal views to forest trails, and appreciate the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels. Over 1,800 runners have used komoot to explore Ea's varied terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the sea or coast?
A: Absolutely! Ea is known for its stunning coastal beauty. Many running routes provide refreshing sea air and panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31894464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Laga Beach – Laga Beach loop from Ibarrangelu&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering spectacular coastal vistas throughout the run.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a quick 5k run?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058597725/litanies-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Litanies Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4 km (3.3 miles) route that can typically be completed in about 42 minutes. It offers varied terrain, making it a great option for a quick yet engaging run.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Ea?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn often provide mild temperatures and lush scenery, ideal for jogging. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along the coast with its refreshing sea breeze.

Q: Are there any natural parks or reserves near Ea that are good for running?
A: Yes, Ea is nestled within the &lt;strong&gt;Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;, a UNESCO-recognized area. This reserve offers a rich diversity of landscapes, including marshes, forests, and coastal paths, all suitable for running and exploring. It's a fantastic place to experience the region's natural beauty while jogging.

Q: Are there any routes that go through or near historical sites?
A: Yes, the region around Ea is rich in history. Some routes may pass by or offer views of historical sites. For example, the area features a historic Roman bridge, and the broader Basque Country has sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1923359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Hermitage and Mundaka Harbor&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer running excursions or visited separately.
tips:
Small town on the coast of Vizcaya (Spain). Since the 16th century, fishing has been the most important traditional activity.
Ea has a river that divides the town into two parts. The houses are located around this stream, forming two long parallel streets. There are 4 bridges that make it easy for us to move from one street to another. One of the bridges, the so-called 'Roman bridge', has become the image of the town and its image appears on the municipality's flag.

Also known as Ermita de la Concepción and Ermita de la Atalaya is a religious building in Spain. It is located in the Biscayan town of Ea. The hermitage is located in a natural watchtower, which is accessed from a small path located in the port of the town.
One of the three main districts of Eako (Bizkaia), located to the east of the municipality. It had its own municipality until the 1887 census. The popular pronunciation of the name is "Bedaru".

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the Sendero de las Letanías trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the village of Ea, which is the starting point for this circular route. Look for designated parking areas within the village, some of which may be free. Arriving earlier in the day is often best to secure a spot, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de las Letanías por la Ermita Talako Ama trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: While the trail is categorized as 'moderate' and is relatively short at just over 5 km, it does feature 'strong slopes' and a 'quite pronounced' descent to the Ermita de la Concepción (Ermita de la Atalaya). Some sections can be uneven or slippery, especially when wet. Beginners should be prepared for varied terrain and some challenging gradients, but it's generally accessible for those with a reasonable fitness level.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll jog on paths bordered by stone walls, dirt tracks, and old 'andabide' paths. There are also stretches alongside roads, some paved and others less used. Be prepared for ascents and descents, including a steep section near the Ermita de la Concepción (Ermita de la Atalaya), which can be challenging, particularly if wet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sendero de las Letanías?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in scenery and history. Key highlights include the charming village of Ea itself, the historic Roman Bridge of Ea, and the Ermita de la Concepción (Ermita de la Atalaya), which offers magnificent coastal views. You'll also pass through serene pine forests, a beautiful ravine with a small bridge, and potentially the hermitage of San Bartolomé with its recreational area.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de las Letanías por la Ermita Talako Ama dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through varied terrain, including some road sections and potentially busy areas in Ea, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash for their safety and the comfort of other trail users and local wildlife. Remember to pack water for your pet and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are often considered the best times to jog this route. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, but it might be warmer and more crowded. Winter can bring mud and potentially slippery conditions, especially on the steeper descents, so exercise caution if visiting during colder, wetter months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sendero de las Letanías trail or to enter the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, as a protected area, it's important to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and follow any local regulations regarding waste disposal and noise.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sendero de las Letanías por la Ermita Talako Ama route is a designated small-route (PR BI 168) and incorporates sections of other local trails. Specifically, the route fully covers the 'Letaniak bidezidorra' trail and also passes through parts of 'Ikuspegiez gozatuz', 'Bizkaiko bira', 'Ura jarraituz', and various sections named 'Urtzaga auzoa', 'Intzueta kalea', and 'Zubizaharra Bekoa'.

faqs.question: What are the trail conditions like during wet weather or winter?
faqs.answer: During wet weather or in winter, certain sections of the trail can become muddy and slippery. The descent to the Ermita de la Concepción (Ermita de la Atalaya), described as 'quite pronounced', may require extra caution, as some steps might be missing or slick. Appropriate footwear with good grip is highly recommended during these times.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or drink in Ea after jogging the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the charming village of Ea offers a few options for refreshments. You'll find local cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can relax and refuel after your jog. These establishments often provide a taste of local Basque cuisine and a welcoming atmosphere.

faqs.question: How can I get to Ea using public transport?
faqs.answer: Ea is accessible by public transport, primarily via local bus services within the Bizkaia region. You would typically look for routes connecting to Ea from larger towns or transport hubs in the area. It's advisable to check the latest bus schedules and routes for Bizkaia to plan your journey effectively.

waypoints.name: Ermita de la Concepción (Ermita de la Atalaya), Ea
